rows.finalText: On "What is the next right step in my calling", this 3-card leans into purpose / calling, with a right path emphasis. With Birds (12) in the past, the reading shows where things already stand, and the picture is already being shaped by communication quality shapes immediate outcomes. Birds often points to too many voices, anxious discussion. Coffin (8) in Present acts as the fulcrum, so this is the point where tone, timing, or choice can change the sequence. Here, Coffin emphasizes the part of the old path that has already finished, and the rest that is needed before anything genuinely new can take root. What develops in Future through Scythe (10) points to urgent prioritization is required, provided the central pressure is handled honestly. Here, Scythe emphasizes the necessary cut, refusal, or clean boundary that separates vocation from habit, urgency. What ties the vocational pattern together is the link between Birds (12) and Scythe (10): a blend of dialogue and cut, asking you to favor concise, clarifying dialogue while staying alert to reactive or harsh decisions. Coffin (8) is the real hinge in this spread. Coffin colors your calling through the part of the old path that has already finished, and the rest that is needed before anything genuinely new can take root. Birds (12) with Scythe (10) keep emphasizing a blend of dialogue and cut, asking you to favor concise, clarifying dialogue while staying alert to reactive or harsh decisions. Allow closure and protect recovery time so Scythe (10) can land as lived experience rather than just possibility.
